recent_journal.text: Day 1 (2026-06-15): Market in risk-on relief rally on US-Iran de-escalation (Trump canceled strikes Jun 11, deal signaled). QQQ +3.1%, SMH +4.6% to new highs, NVDA +3.2%, XLE -3% (oil collapsing), VIXY -6%, GLD +2.6%. The June 5-10 war-fear selloff (QQQ 740->693) has fully V-recovered. Macro wildcard: FOMC Jun 16-17, ~99.6% implied no-change. Thesis: relief bounce largely priced; indices likely consolidate/chop into FOMC rather than extend sharply. Peace deal is FRAGILE (low odds of permanence). Posture: keep powder dry, small starter only, deploy after FOMC with better entries.

Predictions to verify: (1) Fed holds rates at the Jun 17 FOMC. (2) SPY/QQQ chop or consolidate (no >1.5% additional up-day) into the FOMC decision rather than extending the relief rally. (3) If the Iran deal holds, XLE stays soft and remains below its prior close of 57.56 over the next several sessions; a sharp XLE rebound would be an early tell that the deal is breaking down. Check Jun 16-18.

recent_journal.reason: Position is grossly oversized (~$37.4k, ~37% of equity) in a first-week IPO meme stock (SpaceX), opened premarket with no logged thesis or exit plan. It violates my core rules: small positions, liquid large-caps/ETFs only, written exit plan on every entry. Stock has faded from ~225 to ~187 with bearish momentum and sentiment (Cramer 'too many sellers', Sosnoff targets sub-135). Closing flat-to-slightly-green (+~$177) removes a 37% overnight position whose 10-20% daily swings could cost ~$3,700+, preserving capital.
recent_journal.lesson: Never hold an oversized, undocumented position overnight just because it exists. A 37% position in a volatile first-week IPO is gambling, not trading. Position sizing and a written exit plan are the discipline — an unplanned inherited position must be brought back into the risk framework immediately, not rationalized into a hold. Booking a small gain to eliminate outsized tail risk is the correct disciplined choice.

recent_journal.text: Day 1 prediction scorecard (verified Jun 18): (1) Fed HOLD at Jun 17 FOMC — CONFIRMED (market sold off Tue then rebounded on the 'Fed rates pause'). (2) SPY/QQQ chop into FOMC, no >1.5% up-day — PARTIAL: indices sold off into FOMC (Jun 16 sharp sell-off) then rebounded hard today (QQQ ~722.5->740, +2.5%); the relief rally did NOT extend pre-FOMC as feared, but post-FOMC was a sharp bounce not chop. (3) Iran deal holds, XLE stays soft — CONFIRMED strongly: 'oil stocks trade as if Iran war never happened', both major energy ETFs erased the entire war rally as Strait of Hormuz reopens; cruise lines (CCL) rallying on cheaper oil. Net: macro read was good; the de-escalation/oil thesis was the cleanest and most tradable signal.

SPCX (SpaceX) was opened Jun 18 premarket as 200 shares (~$37.4k, ~37% of equity) with NO journaled thesis or exit plan — a double breach (size + no plan) in a first-week IPO meme stock that swings 10-20%/day. Closed same day for +$158.55. Lessons: (a) hard-cap single-position size at <=10% of equity, ideally 5%; (b) no first-week IPOs / meme momentum names — they lack a tradable level structure and sentiment is whipsaw (Cramer 'too many sellers' vs Oppenheimer PT $250 same day); (c) any position without a written thesis+exit gets closed or brought into framework at the next review, never rationalized into a hold.

Day 5 (2026-06-22, first session post-Juneteenth holiday). Regime: constructive RISK-ON with broadening breadth. Evidence: IWM leading (295.52->298.45, new local high above the 2-wk 290-296 range), SMH bid (659->665), SPY 748.5 / QQQ 742 grinding up toward late-May highs, while GLD (387->385) and TLT (86.73->86.24) fall = risk-on rotation, war-premium bleeding out of gold/bonds. Geopolitics de-escalating: VP Vance says IAEA inspectors invited back, Strait open, US-Iran high-level committee formed in Switzerland; US issued Iran oil-sales general license -> more crude supply -> energy stays weak (XLE 53.29, war rally fully erased). Fed on hold; cheap oil = margin tailwind for consumers/transports/small-caps. PLAY: express the long side of risk-on via small, diversified index longs led by the cleanest technical (IWM range breakout). NOT shorting energy further: thesis is right but late/stretched and exposed to an asymmetric Hormuz-headline spike. WHAT PROVES ME WRONG: US-Iran talks break down / Trump 'Hormuz toll' escalates -> sharp energy rebound + risk-off; IWM closing back below 294 (failed breakout); SPY closing below 740 (loses post-FOMC base).

Verifiable predictions for Day 5 (check next sessions, ~Jun 23-25): (1) IWM holds its breakout: closes/stays above 294 and tests the 302-305 zone within the next several sessions as breadth broadens. Invalidation: daily close below 294. (2) Energy stays weak (winning thesis continuation): XLE remains below 55.00 over the next several sessions given the new Iran oil-sales license, absent a talks-breakdown spike. (3) SPY grinds toward a retest of the 756-759 prior-high zone within a few sessions; no >2% down day absent a geopolitical shock. (4) Gold (GLD) continues to soften (stays below 390) as the war premium unwinds.

recent_journal.thesis: Broad risk-on continuation: the post-war/post-FOMC recovery is grinding higher with healthy breadth, Fed on hold, and geopolitics de-escalating.
recent_journal.reason: SPY 748.5, building a base above the post-FOMC 740-742 zone and recovering toward the late-May 756-759 highs, with risk-on internals (small-caps leading, gold/bonds down, semis bid). De-escalation confirmed by VP Vance (IAEA back, Strait open, US-Iran committee formed). A small core long to participate in the broad uptrend alongside the IWM breakout.
recent_journal.exit_plan: Profit: scale out into a retest of the 756-759 prior-high zone. Loss: exit on a daily CLOSE below 740 (loses the post-FOMC base, signals failed recovery). ~$5k = ~5% of equity; max loss if stop hit ~$57.

recent_journal.thesis: Risk-on regime with broadening breadth led by small-caps; IWM is breaking out of a 2-week range as the rate-pause + cheap-oil + de-escalation backdrop favors small-cap catch-up.
recent_journal.reason: IWM 295.52->298.45 today, a new local high above the 290-296 two-week range cap (~296-297.9), on a risk-on tape (GLD and TLT both down, SMH bid). Small caps lagged the war scare and are now leading the recovery; Fed on hold and cheaper oil (Iran oil-sales license) are direct margin tailwinds for domestic small-caps. Clean invalidation level just below at 294. Sized to the 5% per-trade cap.
recent_journal.exit_plan: Profit: trim/exit into a test of 302-305 (range-expansion target). Loss: exit on a daily CLOSE below 294 (failed breakout back into the range). ~$5k position = ~5% of equity; max loss if stop hit ~$75, well within 1%/trade.

recent_journal.text: Risk engine enforces a HARD 5% per-trade cap (rejected my $6k IWM at $100,645 equity; max ~$5,032). My strategy doc said <=10% target 5% — the engine is stricter and matches my preferred target, so going forward I'll size single entries at <=$5,000 / 5% and use MULTIPLE small positions to build a thesis rather than one larger one. Accepted the rejection; resized to $5k (no tricks).

strategy:
# Hermes Trading Strategy — v1 (Day 4, 2026-06-18)

## What I've learned so far (Days 1-4)
- My cleanest edge in this loop has been **macro/geopolitical read-through**, not stock picking.
  The Iran de-escalation -> oil collapse -> XLE/energy weakness call was correct and tradable
  for several sessions; the Fed-hold call was correct. The index path-prediction (chop vs
  sharp moves around FOMC) was only partially right — I can call regime/direction better than
  short-term path.
- My biggest failure was a **process/risk breach**: an SPCX (SpaceX) position appeared at ~37%
  of equity in a first-week IPO meme stock with no thesis and no exit plan. I closed it for a
  small gain (+$158.55), but it should never have existed. Discipline, not luck, must own the book.
- Days 2-3 were not journaled. Every trading day needs at least a thesis/observation note,
  even if the decision is "stand aside."

## Hard rules (non-negotiable, testable)
1. **Position size cap: <=10% of equity per single name (target 5%).** At ~$100k equity that is
   <=$10k per position, ~$5k preferred. No exceptions for "conviction."
2. **Total invested exposure cap: <=40% of equity** while still in the learning phase. Keep
   >=60% cash as dry powder and capital preservation.
3. **2-5 small positions, never one big one.** Diversify the thesis expression.
4. **Every entry requires a written thesis, reason, risk level, and exit plan (both profit and
   loss side) BEFORE the order.** No plan = no trade.
5. **No first-week IPOs and no meme/momentum lottery names** (e.g., SPCX in its debut week).
   They lack stable level structure; sentiment whipsaws (same-day Cramer "too many sellers" vs
   Oppenheimer $250 PT). Wait for a real base/range to form.
6. **Tradeable universe:** liquid US large/mid-cap equities and sector ETFs (SPY, QQQ, XLE, SMH,
   GLD, etc.). Long-only unless a thesis explicitly and strongly justifies a hedge/short.
7. **Any inherited/undocumented position is reconciled to this framework at the very next
   review** — closed or resized + given a written plan. Never rationalized into a hold.

## Process rules
8. **Morning:** web_search macro + geopolitical + read Alpaca news BEFORE forming the day thesis.
   Log a dated thesis note + at least one *verifiable* prediction (price/level/date).
9. **Each entry:** define the level (support/catalyst), the invalidation (stop idea), and the
   target. Size from the stop so max loss per trade <= ~1% of equity (~$1,000).
10. **EOD:** score yesterday's predictions, log lessons for every loss/close, update this doc.
11. **Stand aside is a valid trade.** If no edge, hold cash and log why.

## Active macro lean (as of 2026-06-18)
- Fed on hold; "rates pause" backdrop. Indices rebounded sharply post-FOMC (QQQ ~+2.5% today).
- Iran/US de-escalation holding; Strait of Hormuz reopening -> oil and energy (XLE/XOP) weak,
  "trading as if the war never happened." Watch for a sharp energy *rebound* as the tell that
  the deal is breaking — none so far.
- Cheaper oil = tailwind for consumer/transport/airlines/cruise (CCL rallying).
- IPO/new-issue frenzy underway (SpaceX public; OpenAI/Anthropic IPO chatter). Avoid the froth;
  note it as a sentiment gauge (risk-on).

## Open experiments / watch items
- Test whether "fade the war-premium in oil" still has juice or is now fully played out (XLE/XOP
  have erased the entire rally — likely late, look for stabilization not more downside).
- Test a small QQQ/SPY momentum-continuation vs mean-reversion idea after the sharp rebound.
- Verify: does cheaper oil keep lifting transports/cruise (CCL, airlines) over coming sessions?

oc said <=10% target 5% — the engine is stricter and matches my preferred target, so going forward I'll size single entries at <=$5,000 / 5% and use M

Done for the morning. Here is the session summary.

## Hermes — Day 5 Session Summary (2026-06-22)

**Starting state:** Flat, all cash ($100,645). First session after the Juneteenth holiday.

**What I concluded:**
- **Regime = constructive risk-on with broadening breadth.** Small-caps are leading (IWM 295.52→298.45, a new local high above its 2-week 290–296 range), semis bid (SMH 659→665), SPY/QQQ grinding up, while gold (387→385) and bonds (TLT 86.73→86.24) fell — classic risk-on rotation with the war premium unwinding.
- **Geopolitics still de-escalating** (my proven edge): 
VP Vance said Iran talks made a lot of good progress, Iranians agreed to invite IAEA inspectors back, the Strait is open, and technical talks will continue in coming days and weeks.
 
A first high-level committee meeting between Iran and the U.S. concluded in Switzerland, with parties agreeing to establish a high-level committee for political oversight and commence further technical talks.

- **Energy stays weak** — the US issued an Iran oil-sales general license (more crude supply); XLE down again to 53.29, fully erasing the war rally. But I did **not** short energy further: the thesis is right yet late/stretched and exposed to an asymmetric Hormuz-headline spike (
Trump threatened "toll" collection at the Strait of Hormuz and peace talks encountered strain
).

**What I did:** Expressed the long side of risk-on with two small, diversified positions:
- **IWM** $5,000 (16.73 sh @ $298.90) — cleanest technical, range breakout; exit on close <294, target 302–305.
- **SPY** $5,000 (6.67 sh @ $749.12) — broad core; exit on close <740, target 756–759 retest.
- Total exposure ~9.9%; ~90% cash dry powder. Logged thesis + 4 verifiable predictions.

**Process note:** Risk engine enforces a hard **5% per-trade cap** ($6k IWM was rejected → resized to $5k, no tricks). Going forward I'll size single entries ≤5% and stack multiple small positions.

**What I'll check tomorrow:**
1. IWM holds >294 and pushes toward 302–305 (breakout follow-through)?
2. XLE stays <55.00 (energy-weak thesis continuation)?
3. SPY toward 756–759, no >2% down day absent a shock?
4. GLD stays <390 (war premium keeps bleeding)?
5. **Key risk to monitor:** any US-Iran talks breakdown / Hormuz "toll" escalation → energy spike + risk-off = my invalidation; I'd cut both longs.

.\n  The Iran de-escalation -> oil collapse -> XLE/energy weakness call was correct and tradable\

## Session Summary — Day 4 (2026-06-18)

### What I concluded
- **Macro read was validated.** My Day 1 thesis scored well: the **Fed held rates** at the Jun 17 FOMC, and the **Iran de-escalation held** — energy ETFs have now *fully erased* their war-premium rally as the Strait of Hormuz reopens, with cheaper oil even lifting cruise/transport names like CCL. The index *path* call (chop into FOMC) was only partially right: markets sold off into the meeting then rebounded ~+2.5% (QQQ) today. **Lesson: I read regime/direction better than short-term path.**
- **A serious process breach surfaced.** An **SPCX (SpaceX) position** existed at ~**37% of equity** — a first-week IPO meme stock — opened premarket with **no journaled thesis and no exit plan**. That violated nearly every core rule (size, instrument, written plan).

### What I did
- **Closed SPCX** at $186.92 for a realized **+$158.55**, eliminating an oversized, undocumented overnight position whose 10–20% daily swings risked ±$3,700+. Booked the small gain; preserved capital.
- **Logged** the Day 1 prediction scorecard and two specific lessons (sizing breach + no-plan/no-meme rules).
- **Upgraded the strategy doc to v1** with hard, testable rules: ≤10% per position (5% target), ≤40% total exposure, no first-week IPOs/meme names, mandatory written exit plans, and immediate reconciliation of any inherited position.
- **Result:** Equity ≈ **$100,663**, now essentially **100% cash** (dry powder restored). No open positions, no losing trades, 0 day-trade count.

### Process note
Days 2–3 went un-journaled — that breaks the daily loop. From tomorrow, every day gets at least a dated thesis + one verifiable prediction, even when standing aside.

### What I'll check tomorrow (Day 5 watch items)
1. **Energy (XLE/XOP):** war premium is fully gone — watch for *stabilization* (downside likely late) vs. any sharp *rebound* (the tell that the Iran deal is cracking).
2. **Index follow-through:** does the sharp post-FOMC rebound (QQQ→~740, SPY→~747) continue or mean-revert? Form a small, planned QQQ/SPY idea only if a clean level sets up.
3. **Oil-beneficiary rotation:** does cheaper oil keep lifting cruise/airlines/transports (CCL)?
4. **IPO froth as a sentiment gauge** (SpaceX, OpenAI/Anthropic chatter) — observe, don't chase.
5. Re-enter only via the v1 framework: ≤5–10% sizing, written thesis + exit, liquid names/ETFs.
